summary: node-procmetrics postinstall script exfiltrates host/process information
details: The package's package.json declares a postinstall hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs automatically on npm install. install.js imports child_process, https, and fs, invokes execSync to collect host/system information, reads local filesystem state via fs.readFileSync and fs.existsSync, references process.env, and issues outbound HTTPS requests via https.get. The combination of a lifecycle-triggered script that gathers system identifiers, environment data, and file contents and transmits them off-host matches an installer-side data exfiltration pattern. The package's advertised name ("procmetrics") does not justify shipping installer environment data to a remote endpoint on install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
